content.text:
<session_context>
This is the Gemini CLI. We are setting up the context for our chat.
Today's date is Saturday, June 13, 2026 (formatted according to the user's locale).
My operating system is: darwin
The project's temporary directory is: /Users/ingo/.gemini/tmp/cwd-66
- **Workspace Directories:**
  - /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/.build/refresh/gemini-cli/2-3_task-list-20260613T140557/cwd
- **Directory Structure:**

Showing up to 200 items (files + folders).

/Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/.build/refresh/gemini-cli/2-3_task-list-20260613T140557/cwd/
└───.gemini/
    └───settings.json


</session_context>

Use your write_todos tool to create exactly three todo items, in this order: 'read README', 'summarize README', 'reply done'. Then work them one at a time: for each item call write_todos to set it in_progress, then call write_todos again to set it completed, before moving to the next. Keep everything in this one session — do not spawn any subagent. When all three items are completed, reply with exactly the word: done

thoughts.subject: Initiating The Process
thoughts.description: I've initiated the workflow, and my first step is creating a to-do list as requested. The list contains "read README," "summarize README," and "reply done." I'll be working through these items sequentially, marking each as "in\_progress" then "done" as I complete them.

thoughts.subject: Constructing The Todo List
thoughts.description: I'm now generating the initial to-do list, as instructed. The items, "read README," "summarize README," and "reply done," are being written. I plan to work through them sequentially, marking progress as 'in\_progress' and 'completed' at each step, and using the `write_todos` tool to manage this.

args.strategic_intent: I will create a todo list with three items and then work through them, updating the status of each item as I go, finally replying with "done" when all are complete.
